title: Springerle
ingredients: Scant 5 cups sifted flour (I usually use about 4 ¾ cups)
1 tsp ammonium carbonate (Special order this ingredient. My grandma and great grandma used to buy this at the pharmacy. Can substitute with 1 ½ tsp baking powder but cookies will not be as good.)
instructions: Beat eggs until light. Gradually add in powdered sugar and beat at medium high speed for 20 minutes. Add ammonium carbonate. Add flour and anise oil, knead on lightly floured board until smooth. Roll out ¼ inch thick. Press well-floured wooden springerle molds into dough and cut into squares. Place cookies on parchment lined baking sheets on top of sprinkled anise seeds. Let dry on baking sheets for at least 12 hours. Bake at 275 degrees for 25-30 minutes. Do not open oven door! Remove from oven and cool on racks.
Hide from Mason if you leave the house.
